message:
feat(layout): report pane timeline memory telemetry

bd-25wj7.1

Expose retained-state diagnostics for PaneInteractionTimeline and publish allocation plus retention counters from the pane profile harness so follow-on pruning and versioned-strategy work has structured memory evidence.

Updated tests accordingly.\n\nValidation: cargo test -p ftui-demo-showcase generated_source_is_lazy_but_populates_on_select","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:29:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-25j59","title":"test(markup): add style tag, color alias, error display, and edge case coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:00:39.221065093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T00:04:10.756004704Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T00:04:10.755986860Z","close_reason":"Added 43 tests covering style tags, color aliases, error display, hex/rgb/backslash edge cases","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-25wj7","title":"task(pane-perf): reduce allocation pressure and memory churn in high-frequency interactions","description":"Reduce transient allocations and memory churn during drag-heavy interactions in both hosts.\n\nDeliverables\n- Allocation telemetry and regression guards.\n- Reusable buffers/caches where safe and deterministic.\n- Bounded memory policies for large pane trees.\n\nAcceptance\n- Significant allocation reduction in drag loops.\n- No unbounded cache growth introduced.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Implement scoped performance work based on measured baselines/profiles.\n2) Add benchmark or regression checks aligned with the affected hotspot/budget.\n3) Verify behavior equivalence against golden outputs where optimizations apply.\n4) Emit detailed timing/allocation diagnostics sufficient for CI and local triage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-11T05:34:27.567664244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T04:46:17.987259376Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocations","memory","pane","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7","depends_on_id":"bd-1y0ph","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7","depends_on_id":"bd-23pre","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7","depends_on_id":"bd-2dy6m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7","depends_on_id":"bd-2eqqy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7","depends_on_id":"bd-3jmx7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1059,"issue_id":"bd-25wj7","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Decomposition note: allocation work is no longer treated as generic buffer reuse only. The dominant memory questions are now structural: how much state is retained by checkpoints and versions, what pruning policy preserves deterministic replay while bounding memory, and where structural sharing or small-object storage can remove churn from pane hot paths without changing semantics. The new subtasks under this task are intended to make those memory policies explicit and measurable.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:33:45Z"},{"id":1074,"issue_id":"bd-25wj7","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Detailed child graph created for this task. New child issues: bd-25wj7.1 (allocation/retention telemetry), bd-25wj7.2 (bounded retention and pruning policy), bd-25wj7.3 (structural-sharing and small-object churn reduction). These are the authoritative memory-side subtasks for the asymptotic pane optimization work.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:05Z"},{"id":1077,"issue_id":"bd-25wj7","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Subtree verification contract: memory-side beads must emit artifacted telemetry and regression assertions, not just anecdotal allocation reductions. Future work should treat retained-state accounting, pruning decisions, and churn reductions as user-facing reliability work because memory blowups or opaque retention policies directly degrade responsiveness and debuggability.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:46:13Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-25wj7.1","title":"task(pane-perf): instrument allocation and retention telemetry for replay, checkpoint, and versioned paths","description":"Goal
- Turn pane-memory work into a measured discipline by capturing allocation volume, object retention, checkpoint/version residency, and state-hash overhead across competing execution strategies.

Why this exists
- The performance plan now includes checkpointing and possible persistence. That changes the memory problem from simple transient churn to total retained-state economics.

Deliverables
- Telemetry for transient allocations and retained memory.
- Comparable measurements for baseline, checkpointed, and persistent paths.
- Artifact format suitable for CI or local perf triage.
- Notes on the dominant memory drivers.

Acceptance
- Memory behavior is measurable and attributable by strategy.
- Future retention and pruning work has concrete baselines to optimize against.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Telemetry covers transient allocation counts, retained-state classes, checkpoint/version residency, state-hash overhead, and strategy-specific memory footprints.
2) The telemetry path is regression-tested and emits structured artifacts/manifests reusable by CI, local debugging, and later pruning-policy work.
3) Logs and reports make the dominant memory drivers understandable to humans, not just raw counters.
4) Measurements are deterministic enough to compare baseline, checkpointed, persistent, and policy-selected strategies meaningfully.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:38:17.089183901Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","updated_at":"2026-03-14T04:58:02.765526117Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocations","memory","pane","perf","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:46.204681247Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-25wj7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:38:17.089183901Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1067,"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.1","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Background: this bead exists so future sessions do not talk vaguely about memory again. The core question is not only how many allocations happen but what must remain resident to buy replay/undo performance and how much that costs. The telemetry should make that trade space explicit.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:04Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-25wj7.1","title":"task(pane-perf): instrument allocation and retention telemetry for replay, checkpoint, and versioned paths","description":"Goal
- Turn pane-memory work into a measured discipline by capturing allocation volume, object retention, checkpoint/version residency, and state-hash overhead across competing execution strategies.

Why this exists
- The performance plan now includes checkpointing and possible persistence. That changes the memory problem from simple transient churn to total retained-state economics.

Deliverables
- Telemetry for transient allocations and retained memory.
- Comparable measurements for baseline, checkpointed, and persistent paths.
- Artifact format suitable for CI or local perf triage.
- Notes on the dominant memory drivers.

Acceptance
- Memory behavior is measurable and attributable by strategy.
- Future retention and pruning work has concrete baselines to optimize against.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Telemetry covers transient allocation counts, retained-state classes, checkpoint/version residency, state-hash overhead, and strategy-specific memory footprints.
2) The telemetry path is regression-tested and emits structured artifacts/manifests reusable by CI, local debugging, and later pruning-policy work.
3) Logs and reports make the dominant memory drivers understandable to humans, not just raw counters.
4) Measurements are deterministic enough to compare baseline, checkpointed, persistent, and policy-selected strategies meaningfully.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:38:17.089183901Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","updated_at":"2026-04-25T16:30:20.785633676Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocations","memory","pane","perf","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:46.204681247Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-25wj7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:38:17.089183901Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1067,"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.1","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Background: this bead exists so future sessions do not talk vaguely about memory again. The core question is not only how many allocations happen but what must remain resident to buy replay/undo performance and how much that costs. The telemetry should make that trade space explicit.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:04Z"},{"id":1099,"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.1","author":"WindyBeaver","text":"Landed first instrumentation slice for allocation + retention telemetry. Code now exposes PaneInteractionTimeline::retention_diagnostics() with retained entry/cursor/redo counts, checkpoint residency, baseline/checkpoint snapshot node counts, extension payload bytes, operation payload bytes, and conservative retained-byte estimates. pane_profile_harness now emits allocation_diagnostics from stats_alloc plus retention_diagnostics in manifest JSON and run log lines. Proof run: cargo bench -p ftui-layout --bench pane_profile_harness -- --out-dir target/pane-profiling/bd-25wj7-allocation-retention --iterations 8 --warmup-iterations 1 --operations 32 --leaf-count 32 produced deterministic allocation counters for iteration 0 and 7: allocations=340, deallocations=307, reallocations=3, bytes_allocated=116196, bytes_deallocated=108422, bytes_reallocated=2912; retained_snapshot_count=3, retained_snapshot_node_count=189, retained_leaf_payload_bytes=642, estimated_total_retained_bytes=27170. Remaining scope before full acceptance: add comparable persistent/versioned strategy measurements and summarize dominant memory drivers across all strategies.","created_at":"2026-04-25T16:29:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-25wj7.2","title":"task(pane-perf): design bounded retention and pruning policy for checkpoints, versions, and caches","description":"Goal
- Define and implement a deterministic retention policy that caps memory growth from checkpointing, persistent versions, and any pane perf caches without breaking replay or debugging requirements.

Why this exists
- Architectural replay-speed improvements are only operationally safe if they come with explicit memory ceilings and pruning behavior.

Scope
- Identify the retained-state classes that need caps.
- Define pruning rules and their invariants.
- Ensure debugging, replay, and state-hash guarantees remain understandable under retention pressure.
- Document fallback behavior when the budget is exhausted.

Deliverables
- Retention-policy specification.
- Implementation of caps and pruning behavior.
- Tests for pruning edge cases and deterministic fallback.
- Memory-before/after evidence.

Acceptance
- Memory usage is bounded by explicit policy rather than hope.
- Policy behavior is deterministic, observable, and regression-tested.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Unit and integration tests cover pruning edge cases, deterministic budget enforcement, replay/debug fallback behavior, and state-hash preservation under retention pressure.
2) The policy emits detailed telemetry and human-readable logs for retained-state classes, pruning decisions, budget exhaustion, and fallback events.
3) Operators and developers can force conservative behavior during debugging and understand exactly what data may be discarded by policy.
4) Memory ceilings are explicit, regression-tested, and tied to user-facing responsiveness and operational safety rather than hidden cache growth.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:38:22.084765057Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","updated_at":"2026-03-14T04:57:39.068665002Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["memory","pane","perf","policy","retention"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:56.239710160Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:41:01.231576568Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-25wj7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:38:22.084765057Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-25wj7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:51.234173659Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1068,"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.2","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Reasoning: this bead is the operational guardrail for the whole asymptotic optimization effort. Bigger speedups are not worth much if they quietly turn pane interactions into an unbounded retained-memory system. The right output here is a policy future operators and developers can explain, not just a cache that seems fine.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-25wj7.3","title":"task(pane-perf): reduce pane-core churn via structural sharing and small-object hot-path storage","description":"Goal
- Apply memory-focused hot-path changes such as structural sharing, compact touched-node storage, or other usually-small representations where the data justifies them.

Why this exists
- Once allocation/retention telemetry is available, this bead owns the concrete hot-path memory reductions that do not require a broader strategy shift on their own.

Deliverables
- One-lever memory reductions based on measured hotspots.
- Benchmarks and telemetry showing reduced churn or retained size.
- Proof notes showing semantics remain unchanged.

Acceptance
- Memory churn or retained state falls on the targeted path.
- No opaque or unbounded storage policy is introduced.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Any hot-path memory change is justified by measured churn or retained-state evidence, not aesthetic preference.
2) Unit and integration tests cover representation invariants, fallback behavior, and semantic equivalence where compact storage or sharing alters internal structure.
3) Detailed telemetry and logs explain what memory was saved, on which workloads, and with what trade-offs.
4) No optimization in this bead can bypass the bounded-retention policy or make rollback/debugging materially harder.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:38:27.104247452Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","updated_at":"2026-03-14T04:58:02.724873184Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["memory","pane","perf","smallvec","structural-sharing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:41:11.270263868Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-25wj7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:38:27.104247452Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-25wj7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:41:06.280553804Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1069,"issue_id":"bd-25wj7.3","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Intent: keep this bead grounded. It should only absorb changes that clearly target measured pane-memory hotspots and that remain understandable in the presence of checkpointing or persistence. The existence of small-object techniques is not enough by itself; the profile must demand them.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:40:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-260gd","title":"Edge-case tests for ftui-core event.rs (crossterm mapping + key helpers)","description":"Add unit tests for crates/ftui-core/src/event.rs focusing on:\n- KeyEvent helpers (is_char/ctrl/alt/shift/super_key)\n- Event::from_crossterm mapping for representative crossterm events: key (including modifiers/kind), mouse (buttons + drag/scroll + modifiers), resize, paste, focus.\n\nKeep tests behind cfg(all(not(target_arch = "wasm32"), feature = "crossterm")).\n\nGates: cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleRobin","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:37:32.926067376Z","created_by":"PurpleRobin","updated_at":"2026-02-10T04:44:36.124352996Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T04:44:36.124331195Z","close_reason":"Added event.rs crossterm mapping/key edge-case tests; gates: fmt/clippy -D warnings/test (ftui-core --features crossterm)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
